mechanism_narrative: Sustained quarterly growth in real GDP compresses equity risk premiums by confirming corporate earnings expansion, driving positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: Walk-forward Rank IC remains below 0.03 over any 2-year rolling window, or 20-day forward returns in quarters with positive GDP growth are not significantly higher than baseline.

mechanism_narrative: A decline in the unemployment rate below 4.5 percent signals labor market tightness that boosts consumer spending and corporate revenues, generating positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: SPX produces negative 20-day returns in more than 60 percent of instances where UNRATE drops below 4.5 percent.

mechanism_narrative: Concurrent increases in housing starts and retail sales volumes indicate synchronized consumer and business confidence, which lifts equity valuations over the subsequent 60 trading days through reinforced earnings expectations.
failure_condition: Joint monthly increases in HOUST and RSAFS fail to predict positive 60-day forward returns in a statistically significant majority of observations.

mechanism_narrative: Rising federal funds rates during an active tightening cycle increase discount rates on future cash flows and tighten financial conditions, producing negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: 20-day forward returns remain positive or neutral in more than 50 percent of months where FEDFUNDS increases during a documented tightening cycle.

mechanism_narrative: Month-over-month acceleration in industrial production reflects expanding manufacturing capacity and supply chain efficiency, which supports corporate margins and drives positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 5 trading days.
failure_condition: Rank IC for INDPRO changes against 5-day forward SPX returns stays below 0.02 over any 3-year rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: Nonfarm payrolls exceeding a 3-month cumulative gain of 150,000 jobs signals robust aggregate demand growth, which lifts equity valuations and yields positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 60 trading days.
failure_condition: SPX generates negative 60-day returns in more than 55 percent of months where the 3-month PAYEMS cumulative change surpasses 150,000.

mechanism_narrative: Simultaneous rises in total nonfarm payrolls and declines in the unemployment rate confirm a self-reinforcing labor market recovery, which reduces equity risk premiums and produces positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: The joint signal of rising PAYEMS and falling UNRATE does not yield a positive predictive Rank IC greater than 0.05 for 20-day forward SPX returns.

mechanism_narrative: Consumer price index levels above 315 combined with rising federal funds rates indicate persistent inflationary pressure that erodes real equity valuations and triggers negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next 60 trading days.
failure_condition: 60-day forward SPX returns are positive in more than 60 percent of observations where CPIAUCSL exceeds 315 and FEDFUNDS rises concurrently.
